zh.lifeSummary: 當John Hewitt於1460年2月11日出生在英國英格蘭艾可格亞特 布羅姆利時，他的父親Edward Hewitt14歲，母親Rachell Pope15歲。他於1483年與Joan Harding在英國英格蘭艾可格亞特 布羅姆利結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1540年在他的家鄉享年80歲。

nameMeanings.name: Hewitt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: from the Middle English personal name Hewet, Huet, Hughet, Howet, pet forms of Hugh (Middle English Hewe, Hue; see Hugh and compare Hew ). The spelling Hughet stood for both Huet and the synonymous Huget; see Huggett . This surname has also been established in Ireland since the 14th century.

habitational name from Hewitts in Chelsfield or Hewitts in Willesborough (both Kent), both named from Old English hīewett ‘cutting’, denoting a place where trees had been cut down. Here there lived families called de la Hewatte (1270), de la hewett (1301), and atte Hewete (1338). The name may also be topographic for someone who lived in a newly made clearing in a wood.

variants.name: Hewitson
meanings: English (northern): patronymic from the personal name Hewet; see Hewitt .

variants.name: Huett
meanings: German (Hütt): status name for someone living in a hut or owning a small shop, Middle High German hütte, or a habitational name from any of several places called Hütt or Hütte. Compare Hutt .

variants.name: Hugh
meanings: English, Welsh, and Scottish (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Hue, from ancient Germanic Hugo (ultimately derived from hugi- ‘mind, spirit’). This was a very popular name in England after the Norman Conquest, partly due to the reputation of Saint Hugh of Lincoln (1140–1200), who was born in Burgundy and who established the first Carthusian monastery in England.
Scottish and Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic personal name Aodh, a derivative of a Gaelic word meaning ‘fire’. See Hughes .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 邱 and 丘, based on their Hakka pronunciation; see   Qiu   1 and 2.

variants.name: Huet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre.
French: from a pet form of the personal name   Hue   ‘Hugh’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When John Hewitt was born on 11 February 1460, in Great Bromley, Essex, England, United Kingdom, his father, Edward Hewitt, was 14 and his mother, Rachell Pope, was 15. He married Joan Harding in 1483, in Great Bromley, Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1540, in his hometown, at the age of 80.

ko.lifeSummary: John Hewitt(이)가 1460년 2월 11일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 그리트 브롬리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edward Hewitt(은)는 14세였고, 그의 어머니 Rachell Pope(은)는 15세였다. 그는 1483년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 그리트 브롬리에서 Joan Harding(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1540년 그의 고향에서 80세의 나이로 사망했다.
